title: Immunoprecipitation Assay
author: Ning Zhang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zgbf3sn

steps.title: To harvest cells in nondenaturing conditions,...
steps.instruction: To harvest cells in nondenaturing conditions, remove media and rinse cells with ice cold 1×PBS

steps.title: Remove PBS and add 0
steps.instruction: Remove PBS and add 0.5 ml ice cold cell lysis buffer to each plate(10cm) and incubate on ice for 5 min

steps.title: native protein
steps.instruction: native protein: Scrape cells off the plate and transfer to microcentrifuge tubes. Keep on ice. Sonicate on ice 3 times for 5 sec each. [denatured condition: scrape cells off the plate and transfer to 2.5 ml tube, boil for 10 min, then add 4 volumes cell lysis buffer, this is the cell lysate. ]

steps.title: Native protein
steps.instruction: Native protein : Microcentrifuge for 10 min at 4 ℃,14000g and transfer the supernatant to a new tube. The supernatant is the cell lysates, store at -80℃.

steps.title: add primary antibody(at the appropriate dilution...
steps.instruction: add primary antibody(at the appropriate dilution as recommended in the product datasheet) to 200ul cell lysate at 1mg/ml. incubate with gentle rocking overnight at 4℃.

steps.title: Add protein A/G agarose beads (10-30ul of 50% bead slurry)
steps.instruction: Add protein A/G agarose beads (10-30ul of 50% bead slurry). Incubate with gentle rocking for 1-3h at 4 ℃.

steps.title: Microcentrifuge for 30 sec at 4℃
steps.instruction: Microcentrifuge for 30 sec at 4℃. Wash pellet five times with 500ul cell lysis buffer. Keep on ice between washes.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet with 20ul SDS sample buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet with 20ul SDS sample buffer. Vortex, then microcentrifuge for 30sec.

steps.title: Heat the sample to 95-100℃ fro...
steps.instruction: Heat the sample to 95-100℃ fro 5 min and microcentrifuge for 1min at 14000g.Or, do not heat.

steps.title: Load the sample on a 4-20% gel for SDS-PAGE
steps.instruction: Load the sample on a 4-20% gel for SDS-PAGE.
